description: metadata.version is "1.0.0" but plugins[0].version and plugin.json are both "4.1.0"; outer metadata version was not updated when plugin advanced from v1 to v4
suggested_fix: Set metadata.version to "4.1.0" to match plugin.json and plugins[0].version

description: "comprehensive Playwright API documentation" — 'comprehensive' is a subjective adjective with no measurable criteria, borderline R01 vague quantifier
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete scope note, e.g., 'For selectors, network interception, auth, and CI/CD patterns, see API_REFERENCE.md'

description: 'writes clean test scripts to /tmp' in description — 'clean' is subjective with no measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Remove 'clean' or replace with a specific constraint, e.g., 'writes self-contained test scripts to /tmp'

description: "playwright": "^1.57.0" allows automatic minor-version updates; a supply-chain compromise in a minor playwright release would silently propagate to all installs
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version "1.57.0" and update intentionally, or enforce via npm ci and committed lockfile

description: marketplace.json metadata.version "1.0.0" diverges from plugin.json version "4.1.0"; version fields across manifests are out of sync
suggested_fix: Align all version fields across plugin.json and marketplace.json metadata and plugins[] entries on every release
